title: Generated Adapter No Direct Edit Guardrails
goal: Implement #361 by making generated CLI adapter surfaces clearly non-authoritative and by preserving the drift check that detects direct edits.
non_goals: Add heavy governance or review workflow.

intent.outcome: Contributors can tell generated adapter code is not the edit surface, and ordinary checks detect direct generated-file edits.
intent.constraints: Stay on master; keep guardrail lightweight; route interface edits to contracts and runtime edits to hand-written code.
intent.latitude: Use marker text, generator output, and focused tests/checks rather than a new governance surface.
intent.escalation: Stop before broad direct-edit policy or package adapter generation.

execution.next_step: Archive this slice and continue with #362 package command surface expansion.
execution.proof: Use proof --changed, generator freshness, contract tooling checks, focused tests, ruff, planning checks, reconcile, summary.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Generated shipped CLI adapters from authoritative package definitions.

required_continuation.activation trigger: After generated surfaces have no-direct-edit markers and freshness guardrails.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Generated adapter files are explicitly non-authoritative and protected by freshness checks.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Planning/memory package adapter migration and multi-adapter projection requirements.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: #360 already supplied direct-edit detection through generated output freshness; #361 made the generated surface self-routing and tested.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Generated adapter output clearly marks itself as derived and routes interface/runtime edits to authoritative surfaces.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: #362 should decide how far generated adapter migration extends to planning and memory command surfaces.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Continue implementing planned work until done.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Proceed from #360 into #361 by making the generated adapter output self-routing and protected against direct edits.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Strengthen generated header and tests instead of introducing heavy governance.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check that command/interface and runtime edit targets are clearly separated.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: generator, generated adapter output, contract tooling/conformance tests, planning state/evidence/review artifacts.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: proof --changed; generator freshness; check_contract_tooling_surfaces; focused tests; ruff; planning checks; reconcile; summary.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Any broad generated-file governance or hook changes.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Package CLI adapter generation and multi-adapter projection requirements.

stop_conditions.stop when: #361 is closed, committed, pushed, and #362 remains planned.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: The guardrail requires a broad policy framework.

context_budget.recoverable later: Package CLI migration and multi-adapter projection.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run and closeout_distillation before archive.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Before adding broad policy or hook machinery.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Add no-direct-edit guardrails for generated CLI surfaces.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Lightweight; direct runtime primitive edits remain allowed; no new adapters.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact marker wording and focused test location.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The change becomes a general governance system.

immediate_next_action: Strengthen generated adapter header and add focused marker tests.
invariants: Do not block hand-written runtime primitive edits.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Generated adapter files are derived; command/interface authority lives in command_adapter_generation.json and runtime behavior lives in hand-written implementation.
open_questions_to_close: Is the generated file clear enough about edit targets?
Does ordinary validation detect direct generated-file edits?
validation_commands: uv run ruff check src tests scripts
completion_criteria: Generated adapter files clearly identify their source contract and regeneration command.
Generated adapter files route command/interface changes and runtime changes to the correct surfaces.
Direct generated-file edits remain detected by checks.
No active execplan remains after archive closeout.

execution_run.what happened: Strengthened generated adapter header with no-direct-edit guidance and added tests asserting source contract, runtime implementation, and regeneration routing remain visible.
execution_run.scope touched: Generator, generated adapter output, focused contract-tooling tests, planning state/evidence/review artifacts.
execution_run.validations run: proof --changed; generator freshness; check_contract_tooling_surfaces; focused contract/conformance tests; workspace CLI tests; ruff; planning doctor; planning surface check; reconcile; summary.
execution_run.result for continuation: #361 closed; #362 remains open for planning/memory command surface expansion.

finished_run_review.scope respected: yes; lightweight marker/test only, no broad governance and no new adapters.
finished_run_review.intent served: yes for #361; no for parent #356, which continues through #362-#363.
finished_run_review.config compliance: dogfooded summary, proof, generator freshness, contract tooling, tests, reconcile, doctor, and planning checks.

proof_report.validation proof: uv run agentic-workspace proof --target . --changed ... --format json selected planning_surfaces and workspace_cli; required and slice-specific checks passed.
proof_report.proof achieved now: generated adapter marker, regeneration freshness, contract tooling, tests, lint, and planning health all passed.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: generated_command_adapters.py has no-direct-edit guidance; tests/test_contract_tooling.py asserts the marker and routing text.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: Continue implementing planned work until done.
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: #361 added generated no-direct-edit marker and tests

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Keep canonical authority in contracts/config/planning/Memory/checks rather than prose adapters or generated executable residue.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Make generated output cheaper and safer to maintain without adding a new first-line surface.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Can a contributor know where to edit without reading chat or broad docs?

closure_check.why this decision is honest: #361 delivered lightweight no-direct-edit guidance; parent #356 still needs package command surface expansion.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Reopen #361 if generated adapter files no longer tell contributors where interface or runtime changes belong.

execution_summary.validation confirmed: proof-selected lanes and slice-specific freshness/check/tests passed.
